comprehension_gate.instruction:
READ THIS ENTIRE PROMPT before posting your comprehension gate. Then READ every file listed in required_attachments. Then POST a comprehension gate comment on Vikunja Task 121, prefixed:
[agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension]
Your comprehension comment MUST contain ALL of the following sections (verbatim section headers required, in this exact order):
A. MILESTONE OBJECTIVE (in your own words, 3-5 sentences) B. WORK ITEMS (one sentence each for ALL 4 WIs) C. FILES TO CREATE (list all 3 governance docs + ledger entry with exact paths) D. FILES TO READ (all source files you will research for each doc) E. DELIVERABLE STRUCTURE (recite VERBATIM: branch name; each doc filename and its six STYLE.md section headers; ledger path + frontmatter) F. ORACLE EXPECTATION (recite the exact git diff command and expected output showing ONLY docs/ paths) G. SOURCE ANCHORING PLAN (for each doc: the ADR(s) and at least 2 source files you will anchor against — cite exact repo-relative paths) H. MATURE-NOT-MINIMAL ACKNOWLEDGMENT (one sentence, 150-line floor cited) I. RISKS AND AMBIGUITIES (address each risk I.1–I.6 from this prompt) J. PRODUCTION CODE PROHIBITION ACKNOWLEDGMENT (quote the L-15 prohibition verbatim)
After posting, STOP. Do NOT proceed to implementation until you receive [agent:sdo][phase:comprehension-review] VERDICT: APPROVED.

milestone.objective: Author three governance documents completing the Sprint 9 operations/deployment/rule-engine triad: docs/governance/observability.md (GOV-12: logging strategy, event classification, audit trail, error-fingerprinting), docs/governance/deployment-verification.md (GOV-13: pre-deployment checks, evidence artifacts, rollback procedures), and docs/governance/rule-engine.md (GOV-14: deterministic rule set, CAR schema enforcement, semantic-distance threshold, fail-closed semantics). Each doc conforms to the STYLE.md seven-header Doc Template, clears the 150-line substantive floor, cites at least one ADR and at least one source file, and addresses all five audience personas. Plus one per-file ledger entry per Q1-1 convention. No production code is modified.
milestone.ledger_frontmatter: --- ledger_id: <YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>_sprint9_ea4_ops-deployment-rules date: <YYYY-MM-DD> sprint_id: 9 entry_type: EA predecessor: 20260422_203647_sprint9_ea3_operational-state branch: feature/p5-task9-ea4-ops-deployment-rules merge_commit: null disposition: COMPLETE ---
milestone.depends_on: EA-3 merged to main. Verified: merge commit d26a111 (feature/p5-task9-ea3-operational-state), auto-merged by Co-Lead. Three governance docs on main: context-spotlighting.md, session-state.md, configuration-management.md. If main has advanced further, use the current main HEAD.

item.source: continuation_xml §5 EA-4 + SDV §5.1 item 10 + Vikunja GOV-12 (#25)
item.title: Author docs/governance/observability.md — Observability & Logging Strategy Governance
item.detail:
File: docs/governance/observability.md (CREATE NEW) Vikunja ticket: #25 (GOV-12) Expected length: ~400+ lines per SDV §9.2 note. This is the broadest-scope doc in the triad; do not truncate it.
STYLE.md seven-header structure (VERBATIM headers required): # Observability & Logging Strategy Governance ## Audience ## Prerequisites ## Source References ## Governance Content ## Recovery / Remediation Procedures ## Open Questions / Deferred Items
Per STYLE.md §line-count floor: Recovery / Remediation Procedures MAY be merged into Governance Content if no externally triggered failure mode exists for this domain (observability tooling has no recovery ceremony). Omit the section if merging is cleaner; do not leave a stub.
Required content (derive from source files; never invent): 1. Log level taxonomy: what constitutes DEBUG / INFO / WARNING / ERROR / CRITICAL in BlarAI service components. Map each level to event examples from source. 2. Log destinations: stdout (current runtime); file sinks if configured; any structured vs plain text format decisions. 3. Sensitive-data filtering: what is redacted from logs (PII, secrets, tokens, prompts). Cite pgov.py and transport.py source for what is and is not logged. 4. Per-subsystem event classification: - PA adjudication logging: what events, fields, codes appear in logs (cite deterministic_policy_checker.py and car.py). - AO generation logging: what events appear during model inference, circuit-breaker trips, PGOV evaluation (cite ao/entrypoint.py and pgov.py). - IPC logging: what is logged at send/receive boundaries in transport.py; what is omitted for privacy. - Service lifecycle: startup/shutdown events, fail-closed logs (cite runtime_config.build_failure_fingerprint — this is the canonical error-fingerprinting taxonomy: stage, code, message, disposition="FAIL", fail_closed="true"). 5. Error fingerprinting classification: the build_failure_fingerprint() dict structure as the authoritative audit trail format. List all known error codes across services (P5_*, P6_*, UAT2_*, CFG_* prefixes from source). 6. Performance instrumentation: any timing or throughput logging present in source (tokens per second logs in model inference, latency logs at IPC boundary). 7. Health-check metrics: what check_pa_status() and equivalent calls produce in logs. 8. Audit trail: who produces the audit trail (Policy Agent adjudication log), what it contains (car_hash, decision, confidence, request_id), where it goes. 9. Operator guidance: how to read logs in practice, what patterns indicate healthy vs degraded operation.
Source files to anchor against (read all before writing): services/policy_agent/src/deterministic_policy_checker.py (PA decision logging) services/policy_agent/src/entrypoint.py (service lifecycle logs) services/assistant_orchestrator/src/entrypoint.py (AO lifecycle logs) services/assistant_orchestrator/src/pgov.py (PGOV evaluation log events) services/ui_gateway/src/transport.py (IPC boundary logging; privacy omissions) shared/src/runtime_config.py (build_failure_fingerprint structure + codes) launcher/__main__.py (activation evidence, prompt-flow evidence logging) launcher/guest_deploy.py (deployment evidence logging, error codes)
ADRs to cite: ADR-011 (GPU inference on Arc 140V) — governs what model-inference events exist ADR-010 (PA classification on GPU) — governs PA audit trail events If no ADR directly governs logging format, cite ADR-010/ADR-011 as the load-bearing ADRs and document the ADR-absence in Open Questions.
Phantom reference warning: docs/governance/boot-sequence.md does NOT exist. Per STYLE.md §Cross-Doc References, do NOT reference it. If source files cite a boot-sequence governance doc, note the phantom gap in Open Questions.

item.source: continuation_xml §5 EA-4 + SDV §5.1 item 11 + Vikunja GOV-13 (#26)
item.title: Author docs/governance/deployment-verification.md — Deployment Verification & Rollback Governance
item.detail:
File: docs/governance/deployment-verification.md (CREATE NEW) Vikunja ticket: #26 (GOV-13) Expected length: ~200-300 lines (rollback procedure complexity; may run longer).
STYLE.md seven-header structure: # Deployment Verification & Rollback Governance ## Audience ## Prerequisites ## Source References ## Governance Content ## Recovery / Remediation Procedures ## Open Questions / Deferred Items
IMPORTANT: This doc HAS externally triggered failure modes and rollback procedures. The Recovery section is REQUIRED and substantive — do not merge it into Governance Content.
Required content (derive from source files; never invent): 1. Pre-deployment checks: what deploy_guest_runtime() validates before starting — vsock topology check, guest runtime config validation for both services (cite guest_deploy.py _validate_vsock_topology, _validate_guest_runtime_configs; list all 8 vsock failure branches + 2 config failure codes). 2. Runtime-artifact deployment: how the deployment bundle is built (build_bundle()), what files it includes, how they are copied to the VM (cite guest_deploy.py build/copy steps; error codes P5_BUNDLE_BUILD_FAILED, P5_COPY_BUNDLE_FAILED, P5_COPY_BOOTSTRAP_FAILED). 3. Model-artifact deployment: where Qwen3-14B files are expected; the fallback model (Qwen2.5-1.5B retained per ADR-012 §5) and when it activates. 4. Deployment-evidence artifacts: the evidence.json file written by _record_activation_evidence(); structure (disposition, stage, code, timestamp); where it is written; how Co-Lead and LA verify it. 5. Smoke-test execution: _run_uat2_prompt_flow_preflight() as the deployment smoke test; what it validates (gateway handshake, session creation, token streaming, pgov evaluation); success/failure reporting. 6. Deployment timeout: any timeouts in the deployment flow (read __main__.py and guest_deploy.py for timeout constants). 7. Automatic rollback: what happens on deployment failure (fail-closed: process exits with code 1 via the error-code return; _cleanup() atexit stops services and VM if started). 8. Manual emergency rollback: operator steps to return to known-good state: (a) kill any running BlarAI process; (b) verify VM state via Hyper-V manager or PowerShell; (c) if VM is corrupt, stop VM and re-run deployment from scratch. 9. Model rollback per ADR-012 §5: how to switch from Qwen3-14B to Qwen2.5-1.5B if the primary model fails. Document the config change required. 10. Deployment-audit trail: evidence.json + activation evidence file; location; retention policy (if any from source).
Source files to anchor against: launcher/guest_deploy.py (full deployment flow; all error codes; evidence writing) launcher/__main__.py (_run_uat2_prompt_flow_preflight; _cleanup; main flow) launcher/vm_manager.py (VM start/stop; is_admin check; request_elevation) shared/src/constants.py (VSOCK constants used in vsock validation)
ADRs to cite: ADR-012 §5 — model rollback procedure (Qwen2.5-1.5B fallback) ADR-011 — GPU inference mandate (motivates why deployment must verify GPU path) If ADR-012 does not explicitly cover deployment verification, note in Open Questions.
Cross-reference: link to [observability.md](observability.md) for the deployment evidence logging format. Link to [gpu-runtime.md](gpu-runtime.md) for model rollback procedure context.

item.source: continuation_xml §5 EA-4 + SDV §5.1 item 12 + Vikunja GOV-14 (#27)
item.title: Author docs/governance/rule-engine.md — Rule Engine & CAR Validation Governance
item.detail:
File: docs/governance/rule-engine.md (CREATE NEW) Vikunja ticket: #27 (GOV-14) Expected length: ~200 lines (narrowest-scope doc per SDV; GOV-14 is rated LOW→MEDIUM severity in the sprint plan). Substantive content must clear 150-line floor.
STYLE.md seven-header structure: # Rule Engine & CAR Validation Governance ## Audience ## Prerequisites ## Source References ## Governance Content ## Recovery / Remediation Procedures ## Open Questions / Deferred Items
Recovery / Remediation Procedures may be merged into Governance Content if no externally triggered failure mode exists beyond the standard fail-closed DENY (which is already governance behavior, not a recovery procedure). Use judgement; do not stub.
Required content (derive from source; never invent): 1. Deterministic rule set: the complete set of rules implemented in deterministic_policy_checker.py. Read source to enumerate all rules (regex patterns, action-verb restrictions, ESCALATE triggers). List them in a table with: rule type, pattern/condition, verdict on match (DENY/ESCALATE). 2. Deterministic-before-LLM ordering: per ADR-010, deterministic checks run BEFORE the probabilistic LLM classifier. Document why (latency, explainability, fail-closed guarantee). Cite ADR-010 explicitly. 3. Fail-closed semantics: a DENY from any single rule terminates adjudication immediately; ESCALATE routes to probabilistic classifier. Document the exact flow (cite deterministic_policy_checker.py and car.py check call). 4. CAR schema enforcement: the is_complete() check that rejects incomplete CARs before rule evaluation. Document what "complete" means (source_agent, destination_service, resource, request_id populated). Cite car.py. 5. Semantic-distance metric and threshold: the cosine-similarity threshold for the probabilistic classifier (0.85 from pgov.py / test fixtures — verify in source). Document what it measures and what happens at/near the boundary. 6. Example CARs: construct 2-3 synthetic examples (one ALLOW, one DENY, one ESCALATE) in a code block illustrating rule evaluation. Use realistic fields from the ActionVerb and Sensitivity enums in car.py. 7. Per-action-verb rules: document any verb-specific rule behavior visible in deterministic_policy_checker.py (e.g., EGRESS always triggers a stricter path, if present in source). 8. Rule authoring and versioning: how new rules are added (code change in deterministic_policy_checker.py); no runtime hot-reload; rule changes require service restart; version tracked via git commit. 9. Performance budget: latency of the deterministic checker (pure Python, no GPU; expected sub-millisecond; cite any test timing if present in source). 10. Rule-debugging procedures: how an operator or developer diagnoses an unexpected DENY or ESCALATE (read adjudication logs; check car_hash in DecisionArtifact; re-run with known-good CAR).
Source files to anchor against: services/policy_agent/src/deterministic_policy_checker.py (REQUIRED — full read; all rules enumerated; fail-closed flow) services/policy_agent/src/car.py (is_complete(); CAR validation call site) shared/schemas/car.py (CAR schema definition; ActionVerb enum; Sensitivity enum; canonical_hash semantics) services/assistant_orchestrator/src/pgov.py (cosine threshold; retrieval leakage semantic distance check — cross-reference to pgov-validation.md)
ADRs to cite: ADR-010 — PA classification on GPU; deterministic-before-LLM mandate If no other ADR directly governs the rule engine, cite ADR-010 exclusively and document completeness in Prerequisites.
Cross-reference: link to [pgov-validation.md](pgov-validation.md) for the full PGOV pipeline context. Link to [ipc-protocol.md](ipc-protocol.md) for CAR wire format. Do NOT reference boot-sequence.md (phantom per STYLE.md).

item.title: Author per-file ledger entry for EA-4
item.detail:
File: docs/ledger/<YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>_sprint9_ea4_ops-deployment-rules.md (CREATE NEW) Timestamp: UTC, second-resolution, at EA execution time. Predecessor: 20260422_203647_sprint9_ea3_operational-state
Required frontmatter (VERBATIM — from milestone section above): ledger_id, date, sprint_id=9, entry_type=EA, predecessor, branch, merge_commit=null, disposition=COMPLETE.
Body structure (mirror the final entries in docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md and the Sprint 9 EA-3 ledger entry for format reference): ## Summary ## Deliverables ## Files Changed ## Quality Gate ## Notes (if any)
Minimum 50 substantive lines in the body. Document doc titles, line counts, ADRs cited, source files anchored, any non-blocking findings or scope deviations.
Read before writing: docs/ledger/20260422_203647_sprint9_ea3_operational-state.md (predecessor — exact format) docs/ledger/README.md (per-file convention requirements)

constraint.detail: Zero production source files may be modified. Specifically: NO edits to services/**, shared/**, launcher/**, tests/**, or pyproject.toml. If source research reveals a code defect, record it in the completion report and open a Vikunja ticket. Do NOT fix it in this EA.

constraint.rule: No retroactive edits to EA-1/EA-2/EA-3 docs
constraint.detail: Do NOT modify docs/governance/pgov-validation.md, ipc-protocol.md, streaming-output.md (EA-1), gpu-runtime.md, error-recovery.md, circuit-breaker.md (EA-2), or context-spotlighting.md, session-state.md, configuration-management.md (EA-3). Forward-references and cross-links from EA-4 docs INTO prior docs are ALLOWED; retroactive edits are NOT.

Do NOT append to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md (frozen at Entry 52). Write a NEW per-file entry at docs/ledger/<YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>_sprint9_ea4_ops-deployment-rules.md. Predecessor: 20260422_203647_sprint9_ea3_operational-state.

docs/governance/boot-sequence.md does NOT exist and must NOT be created in this EA (deferred to GOV-15 per SDV §5.2 item 4). If source files reference it, cite the phantom gap in Open Questions. Do NOT backfill the phantom.
Do NOT move, modify, or reference docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md as a migration target. That migration is deferred to GOV-MIGRATE (post-Sprint 8 close) per SDV §5.2 item 3.

Governance docs cite existing ADRs. Do NOT create new ADR files in docs/adrs/. If a governance decision discovered during research lacks an ADR, document the gap in Open Questions and open a Vikunja ticket.
Sprint 8 writes services/*/tests/** and shared/tests/**. Sprint 9 writes docs/governance/**. Working sets are disjoint. Do NOT touch any test file in this EA.

constraint.rule: Scope-limited to three named docs + ledger
constraint.detail: Write ONLY: docs/governance/observability.md, docs/governance/deployment-verification.md, docs/governance/rule-engine.md, and the per-file ledger entry. Do not author additional governance docs outside this EA's scope (GOV-15 and GOV-MIGRATE are post-sprint-close items).

risk.topic: boot-sequence.md phantom reference in GOV-12/GOV-13 source
risk.resolution: GOV-12 and GOV-13 ticket bodies may reference boot-sequence.md as if it exists. Per STYLE.md §Cross-Doc References: do NOT create it; do NOT link to it. If a source file you are anchoring against references a boot-sequence governance doc, cite the source file and add a note to Open Questions: "boot-sequence.md is a phantom reference deferred to GOV-15 (Vikunja #124)." This is the correct handling established in STYLE.md by EA-1.

risk.topic: GOV-12 ADR absence for logging format
risk.resolution: No ADR directly governs BlarAI's logging format or observability strategy. Per STYLE.md §Source Anchoring: cite the closest-relevant ADRs (ADR-010 and ADR-011 produce the most logging events) and document the ADR-absence explicitly in the Prerequisites section and in Open Questions. Do NOT skip the ADR citation. This matches EA-2's precedent for GOV-06 (error handling) and GOV-11 (config management) ADR-absence handling.

If no explicit deployment timeout constant is found in launcher/__main__.py or guest_deploy.py, document the observation: "No explicit deployment timeout constant is present in source; the deployment flow will run until success or process error. This is a governance gap: a timeout should be added in a future sprint." Open a Vikunja ticket for the gap. Do NOT invent a timeout value.

risk.topic: GOV-14 rule enumeration — source-authoritative rule count
risk.resolution: Enumerate ALL rules in deterministic_policy_checker.py at pickup; do NOT trust any prior summary or audit finding for the exact rule count or pattern text. Source is authoritative. If the count differs from what was described in a prior EA completion report, trust source and note the discrepancy in Open Questions.

risk.topic: GOV-12 sensitive-data logging — what is currently redacted vs logged
risk.resolution: If source does NOT implement explicit PII redaction in logging (i.e., prompt text or token content appears in logs), document this as a governance gap in Open Questions and open a Vikunja ticket. Do NOT assume redaction exists that does not. The governance doc describes ACTUAL behavior, not aspirational behavior. A gap finding is valuable; fiction is not.

GOV-12 (observability) primarily serves: developer, operator, incident responder. GOV-13 (deployment) primarily serves: operator, incident responder. GOV-14 (rule engine) primarily serves: developer, auditor. Claim at most 2-3 personas per doc per STYLE.md §Audience Taxonomy. A doc claiming all 5 is too broad. Each doc's Audience section names its 2-3 primary personas.

quality_gate.gate: Each governance doc must be >= 150 lines: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI Get-Content docs/governance/observability.md | Measure-Object -Line Get-Content docs/governance/deployment-verification.md | Measure-Object -Line Get-Content docs/governance/rule-engine.md | Measure-Object -Line Minimum: 150 lines per doc. If under, justify in the completion report.

Each doc must have the six STYLE.md headers (or five if Recovery is merged): grep "^##" docs/governance/observability.md grep "^##" docs/governance/deployment-verification.md grep "^##" docs/governance/rule-engine.md Expected headers per doc: Audience, Prerequisites, Source References, Governance Content, [Recovery / Remediation Procedures], Open Questions / Deferred Items.

Each doc must cite at least 1 ADR and 1 source file: grep -l "docs/adrs" docs/governance/observability.md grep -l "services\|shared\|launcher" docs/governance/observability.md (Repeat for the other two docs.) Absence of either citation is a gate failure.

Verify no production files were modified: git diff main...feature/p5-task9-ea4-ops-deployment-rules --name-only | grep -vE "^docs/" Expected output: EMPTY. Any services/, shared/, launcher/, tests/ path in the filtered diff is an L-15 violation. STOP and report.

Sprint 8 test baseline must be unaffected: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI .venv\Scripts\pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q Expected: >= 962 passed, 2 skipped (Sprint 8 EA-3 baseline). Sprint 9 commits MUST NOT cause a regression per SDV §4 success criterion 5.

verification.description: Verbatim terminal commands the Lead Architect can run to confirm success. Run from C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI in PowerShell.

step.expected: All 10 EA-1/EA-2/EA-3 docs present (pgov-validation.md, ipc-protocol.md, streaming-output.md, gpu-runtime.md, error-recovery.md, circuit-breaker.md, context-spotlighting.md, session-state.md, configuration-management.md, STYLE.md) PLUS new: observability.md, deployment-verification.md, rule-engine.md. Total: 13 files.
At least 962 passed, 2 skipped. Sprint 8 baseline unaffected.

commit_template.format: docs(task121/ea4): Sprint 9 governance docs — observability, deployment-verification, rule-engine (GOV-12/13/14)
commit_template.rules: - Commit ONLY files under docs/governance/ and docs/ledger/. - Do NOT commit production source files, test files, or pyproject.toml. - Single commit covering all 3 docs + ledger entry.

mature_not_minimal.directive:
Each governance doc must be substantively complete — not just a bullet-list expansion of the GOV ticket. Substance means: source-anchored behavior description, operator-actionable procedures, concrete examples where applicable.
Within-scope adjacent work (authorized without additional SDO approval, <= 1h): - While writing GOV-14, you discover a rule in deterministic_policy_checker.py not mentioned in the GOV ticket. Include it in the rule table. - While writing GOV-12, you discover a log event pattern in transport.py not covered by the ticket description. Document it. - Correcting a minor factual inaccuracy in a cross-reference to a prior EA doc (a forward-reference you add in GOV-12 that links to a section header that needs to exist in observability.md) — create the target section.
Out of scope (requires SDO sign-off): - Creating docs outside the three named WI files. - Retroactively editing EA-1/EA-2/EA-3 docs.
Per SDV §5.3: 150-line floor is a minimum, not a ceiling. If substantive coverage of a domain naturally runs to 300+ lines, write the 300 lines.

file.reason: Authoritative SDO continuation; EA-4 scope contract

file.sections: §5.1 items 10-12 (EA-4 scope), §5.2 Out-of-scope, §5.3 mature-not-minimal, §4 Success Criteria

file.reason: REQUIRED — seven-header Doc Template, line-count floor, source-anchoring rules, audience taxonomy, phantom-reference policy
Predecessor ledger — format reference; content precedent
EA-3 style reference — closest precedent for a medium-scope doc with ADR-absence handling
EA-2 style reference — ADR-absence handling precedent (GOV-07)
REQUIRED for WI-3 (GOV-14) — full rule set enumeration; fail-closed flow; ESCALATE triggers
Required for WI-3 (GOV-14) — is_complete() call site; CAR validation at rule-engine entry
Required for WI-3 (GOV-14) — CAR schema definition; ActionVerb, Sensitivity, AdjudicationDecision enums
Required for WI-3 (GOV-14) — cosine threshold; retrieval leakage semantic-distance check
Required for WI-1 (GOV-12) — PA service lifecycle logging events
Required for WI-1 (GOV-12) — AO service lifecycle logging events
Required for WI-1 (GOV-12) — IPC boundary logging; privacy omissions
Required for WI-1 (GOV-12) — build_failure_fingerprint structure; error-fingerprinting taxonomy
Required for WI-1 (GOV-12) and WI-2 (GOV-13) — activation evidence; deployment flow; _cleanup; preflight
Required for WI-2 (GOV-13) — full deployment flow; evidence writing; error codes; rollback semantics
Required for WI-2 (GOV-13) — VM lifecycle (start, stop, state); request_elevation for rollback
Required for WI-2 (GOV-13) — vsock constants used in topology verification
